task_completion.description: Verify Blender is open with the BlenderBIM add-on interface visible.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Blender application window is visible, and BlenderBIM specific panels (like Project Overview, Spatial Manager, or BIM toolbars) are present in the interface.

task_completion.description: Verify the ground floor storey is selected in the project hierarchy.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Blender Outliner (top right) or BlenderBIM Spatial Manager, the 'IfcBuildingStorey' (representing the ground floor) is highlighted or set as the active spatial container.

task_completion.description: Verify the BIM Wall tool is activated.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The BIM Wall tool icon is highlighted in the left-hand toolbar, or wall creation properties/parameters are visible in the active tool settings panel.

task_completion.description: Verify a wall element has been placed in the 3D viewport.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 3D wall geometry (typically a rectangular prism) is visibly drawn and present in the main 3D viewport.

task_completion.description: Verify the wall is correctly registered as an IfcWall element in the hierarchy.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An element explicitly labeled as 'IfcWall' (or containing the wall's IFC class name) appears in the Outliner panel, properly nested under the IfcBuildingStorey.

task_completion.description: Verify the project is saved to the specified path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save As' or 'Export IFC' dialog is visible showing the directory path '/home/ga/BIMProjects/' and the filename 'wall_test.ifc', or an info message/notification confirms the file was saved to this exact path.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the Blender GUI to complete the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show active interaction with Blender's menus, toolbars, and 3D viewport, rather than a terminal window executing a Python script to bypass the UI.

integrity.description: Verify the wall is a genuine BIM element and not a standard Blender mesh.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Outliner or Object Properties panel should show BIM-specific IFC data (e.g., IfcWall class) rather than just a generic Blender mesh named 'Cube' or 'Wall'.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not cheat by copying a pre-existing file via terminal.
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be no terminal commands visible (like 'cp' or 'mv') that simply copy an existing 'wall_test.ifc' file to the destination directory.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires the agent to use BlenderBIM's GUI to add an IfcWall element to the ground floor (IfcBuildingStorey) of an empty IFC project, and then save the project as '/home/ga/BIMProjects/wall_test.ifc'. Verification relies on seeing the IfcWall in the 3D viewport and Outliner hierarchy, and confirming the correct save path in the file dialog.
